name: Outlaw Chile Rellenos
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Bread crumbs, fresh or store bought
1/4 lb Monterey Jack or Jalapeño cheese, sliced 3/4 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. If using fresh chiles, blister them in the oven or use your favorite method to remove the skins.
2. Cut a small slit in the base of each chile and scoop out the seeds with a tablespoon, discarding them. Canned chiles are already prepared.
3. In a medium-sized mixing bowl, combine the eggs, oregano, powdered garlic, and water until well blended.
4. On a large plate or piece of wax paper, mix together the bread crumbs and Parmesan cheese.
5. Place the flour on a smaller plate or use a piece of wax paper.
6. Butter or grease a cookie sheet.
7. Insert a slice of cheese into each chile.
8. One at a time, coat each chile with flour, dip them in the egg batter, and place them on top of the bread crumbs.
9. Pat the chiles with the bread crumbs to ensure they are fully covered.
10. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
11. Place the chiles on the greased cookie sheet.
12. Drizzle the chiles with butter before baking for 20 minutes.
13. Serve with rice and beans.
¡PROVECHO!
